ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-06-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 12/10/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES SET FOR 12/3/07 @ 10:00AM, 12/5/07 @ 10:30AM, 12/6/07 @ 10:30AM, AND 12/7/07 @ 10:30AM IN DEPT. 220. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND IS DUE BY 11/28/07; DEFENDANTS' COUNTER DEMAND DUE BY 11/30/07. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MAY BE HEARD UP TO 11/28/07. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 10/5/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 10/26/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: DLOK; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE CSR #4280 (206)

FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT NEGATE PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES FOUR THROUGH SIX; NO OPPOSITION TO THIS PORTION OF THE MOTION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO CLAIMS FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL TORT AND PREMISES LIABILITY AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THIS PORTION OF THE MOTION. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES ARE SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
